title: KotaConfig
description: KOTA configuration schema. Loaded from ~/.kota/config.json (global) and .kota/config.json (project). Project-level overrides global. CLI flags override both.

agentModels.description: Per-agent model overrides. Keys are agent names (project or module-contributed); values are model IDs. Takes effect at agent resolve time; invalid model strings are passed through without validation, same as the top-level `model` field.

aliases.description: Prompt aliases — keys that expand into prefix text when starting a message.

approvalTtlMs.description: TTL for pending approval items in milliseconds. Default: 86400000 (24 hours).

autoEnable.description: Tool groups to auto-enable at session start (e.g. ["web", "code"]).

eventBufferSize.description: Number of recent SSE events to retain in the in-memory ring buffer. Clients can query buffered events via GET /api/events or replay them on SSE reconnect with GET /events?since=<timestamp>. Default: 500.

sessionIdleTtlMs.description: How long (ms) a daemon-owned interactive chat session may be idle before it is swept. Default: 300000 (5 minutes).

shutdownGracePeriodMs.description: How long (ms) to wait for active workflow runs to complete before aborting them during shutdown. 0 = drain indefinitely. Default: 60000 (60 s).

failover.description: Model provider failover. When configured, requests fail over to a secondary provider when the primary is detected as unhealthy. Omit to disable (single-provider, no behavior change).

apiKey.description: API key for the fallback provider. Resolved from env if omitted.

baseUrl.description: Base URL for the fallback provider. Uses preset if omitted.

cooldownMs.description: Cooldown before probing the primary again, in milliseconds. Default: 300000.

errorThreshold.description: Number of errors in the sliding window that trigger failover. Default: 5.

model.description: Fallback model string. Falls back to the primary model if omitted.

windowMs.description: Sliding window size in milliseconds. Default: 60000.

foreignModules.description: Foreign-language (out-of-process) modules. Each entry declares a subprocess to spawn and communicate with via KEMP. The protocol contract is owned by the core module types and tests.

anyOf.description: Stdio transport — spawns a subprocess and communicates over stdin/stdout.

cwd.description: Working directory for the subprocess. Relative paths are resolved from the KOTA project root. Defaults to the KOTA project root.

env.description: Additional environment variables for the subprocess.

maxRestarts.description: Maximum number of automatic restart attempts after an unexpected exit. Set to 0 to disable restart. Default: 3.

pingIntervalMs.description: How often (ms) to send a health-check ping. Default: 30000. Set to 0 to disable periodic pings.

pingTimeoutMs.description: Milliseconds to wait for a `pong` response before declaring the subprocess hung and triggering a restart. Default: 5000. Set to 0 to disable pings.

restartBackoffBaseMs.description: Base milliseconds for exponential restart backoff. Default: 2000.

anyOf.description: HTTP transport — connects to an already-running HTTP server that speaks KEMP.

bearerToken.description: Optional bearer token sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every request. Supply a string literal or `{ env: "ENV_VAR_NAME" }` to read from an environment variable. The token is never logged.

url.description: Base URL of the KEMP HTTP server (e.g. "http://localhost:8765").

items.description: Configuration for a single foreign-language module entry.

approvalTimeoutMs.description: TTL in ms for approval requests created in this context. Stored on each queued item.

policies.description: Policy applied at each risk level.

toolOverrides.description: Override policy for specific tool names (bypasses risk classification).

format.description: Output format. "text" is human-readable (default); "json" emits newline-delimited JSON for log aggregators. Overridden by LOG_FORMAT env var.

sampling.description: Sampling settings — allow MCP clients to delegate LLM completions to KOTA. Default: disabled.

enabled.description: Enable the sampling/createMessage handler. Default: false.

modelProvider.description: Model provider configuration for non-Anthropic backends (OpenAI-compat, Ollama, etc.).

crashAlertThreshold.description: Number of restarts within `crashAlertWindowMs` that triggers an `module.crash.alert` notification. Default: 3.

crashAlertWindowMs.description: Rolling window in milliseconds for counting module restarts. Also serves as the alert cooldown — at most one alert per module per window. Default: 600000 (10 minutes).

modules.description: Per-module configuration. Keys are module names, values are module-specific settings.

alertCooldownMs.description: Minimum milliseconds between failure alerts for the same workflow. Default: 0 (no cooldown — every failure fires an alert). Example: 300000 suppresses repeated alerts within 5 minutes.

allowCritical.description: When true (default), critical events bypass quiet hours. Critical: workflow.failure.alert, module.crash.alert.

end.description: Quiet period end in local time, "HH:MM" (24-hour). Spans crossing midnight are supported.

start.description: Quiet period start in local time, "HH:MM" (24-hour).

providers.description: Provider overrides. Keys are service types (e.g. "memory", "knowledge"), values are provider names.

reflection.description: Self-reflection — evaluate response quality before delivering. Default: true.

runsGc.description: Run artifact retention policy for `.kota/runs/`. Applied when `kota workflow gc` is run explicitly. Defaults: retentionDays=7, minKeepPerWorkflow=10.

minKeepPerWorkflow.description: Always keep at least this many recent runs per workflow (default: 10).

retentionDays.description: Delete runs older than this many days (default: 7).

scheduler.description: Scheduler settings for autonomous workflow dispatch.

agentConcurrency.description: Maximum number of agent-step workflows that may run simultaneously. Must be a positive integer. Default: 1 (serial agent dispatch).

codeConcurrency.description: Maximum number of code-only (no agent step) workflows that may run simultaneously. Must be a positive integer. Default: 4.

dispatchWindow.description:
Dispatch window — time-of-day and day-of-week restriction for autonomous workflow triggers.

Affects `runtime.idle` (idle trigger) and `intervalMs` (interval trigger) only. Cron, event, file-watch, and manual triggers are not affected.

days.description: Days on which the window applies. Default: all days. Accepted values: "mon" | "tue" | "wed" | "thu" | "fri" | "sat" | "sun"

end.description: End of allowed window in local time, "HH:MM" (24-hour, exclusive).

start.description: Start of allowed window in local time, "HH:MM" (24-hour).

noAuth.description: Disable bearer-token auth (default: auth enabled). For localhost-only dev use.

showCost.description: Show per-turn cost line in terminal output (default: true). Set to false to suppress.

tracing.description: OpenTelemetry tracing and metrics for workflow execution. Opt-in.

endpoint.description: OTLP HTTP endpoint (e.g. "http://localhost:4318/v1/traces"). Required to enable tracing.

metricsEndpoint.description: OTLP HTTP endpoint for metrics (e.g. "http://localhost:4318/v1/metrics"). Defaults to `endpoint` so a single collector sees both signals.

metricsExportIntervalMs.description: How often to flush metric batches to the exporter. Default: 30 000.

samplingRate.description: Sampling rate between 0 and 1. Default: 1.0 (sample everything).

serviceName.description: Service name reported in traces. Default: "kota".

user.description: User profile — injected into system prompt for personalization.

webhooks.description: Per-workflow webhook secrets for `POST /webhooks/:workflowName`. Keys are workflow names; each entry must have a `secret` string. Keep this in `.kota/config.json` (project-local, gitignored) to avoid committing secrets.

maxStepOutputBytes.description: Maximum step output size in bytes before truncation. Default: 262144 (256 KB). Hard cap: 10485760 (10 MB). Outputs exceeding this limit are replaced with a structured truncation notice.
